summary: The transformation rule appears to be shifting all non-black cells to the right by one position within their respective rows. If a cell is at the far right, it wraps around to the far left of the same row.

In the first image, the yellow squares move right by the number of black squares before them, the red line remains as there are no black squares before it, and the magenta and blue blocks shift similarly.

In the first pair, the yellow diagonal results in all cells below it being filled with yellow. In the second pair, the green diagonal results in all cells below it being filled with green.
